title: Pope Leo's first encyclical criticizes technological messianism
summary: Pope Leo has released his first encyclical, which sharply criticizes technological messianism—the belief that technology will solve all human problems—and warns against replacing humans with artificial intelligence. This encyclical marks a significant intervention by a major religious leader in debates about AI ethics and the societal control of technology, potentially shaping public discourse and policy. The encyclical reportedly acknowledges the Pope's own use of technology even as it condemns unchecked faith in AI, highlighting a tension between embracing and cautioning against technological progress.
context: Technological messianism is the conviction that technology will inevitably lead to positive outcomes and solve all problems. A papal encyclical is a formal letter from the Pope that outlines the Catholic Church's official position on important issues, carrying significant moral authority for believers.
discussion: Commenters debated who should control technology—technologists, users, governments, or religious institutions—with some expressing skepticism about AI hype. Others referenced Peter Thiel's views on the Antichrist and questioned whether AI CEOs suffer from 'AI psychosis.'

references.title: Leo’s first encyclical attacks technological messianism
